title: celtis conferta subsp. conferta
intro: Celtis conferta subsp. conferta, also known as the coastal hackberry or coastal Celtis, is a plant species belonging to the elm family (Celtidaceae) and is native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is valued for its ability to soothe wounds and reduce pain. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving coughs and colds, and supporting digestion. The coastal hackberry can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and treat various health conditions.
